findings.summary: Battery charger/power station with LT3790 buck-boost, ESP32-C3, USB-C PD. Major component classification failure: 105 of 136 components classified as other due to non-standard reference designator prefixes (Cch_, Rch_, Cc_, Ruc, etc).

correct.description: ESP32-C3 missing decoupling correctly flagged in design_observations

incorrect.description: 105 of 136 components (77%) classified as other — components with non-standard prefixes like Cch_, Rch_, Cc_, Ruc, Lch, Qch_, Dch are not recognized as capacitors/resistors/inductors/transistors/diodes
Only 4 capacitors counted in statistics despite ~35 capacitor-type BOM entries with Cc_/Cch_ prefixes
BOM sum (126) does not match total_components (136) — 10 missing, mounting_hole(3) only partially explains gap
LT3790 buck-boost converter not detected as power regulator — classified as other

missed.description: No voltage dividers detected despite many 200K/100K/33K resistors likely forming feedback networks
No current sense detection despite Rch_Ibat (0.013 ohm) and Rch_Iin (0.003 ohm) sense resistors
No charger circuit detection for LT3790 buck-boost charger
ACS712 current sensor IC not detected
MCP4725 DAC not recognized as analog output device

suggestions: Component classifier should parse reference prefixes more flexibly — strip trailing underscores/numbers and match first letter (C=capacitor, R=resistor, L=inductor, Q=transistor, D=diode)
Non-standard prefixes like Cch_, Rch_ are user conventions for hierarchical sheets and should still be classified by first letter
